mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of the Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies cluster management on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free Kubernetes experience with built-in cloud integrations. Key trade-offs include reliance on Linode's infrastructure and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI is required to interact with Linode's API for creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -sL https://github.com/linode/linode-cli/releases/download/v2.0.0/linode-cli-2.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This command downloads and installs the Linode CLI.
> Make the Linode CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version and node count.

```bash

linode-cli lke cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configuring kubeconfig allows you to interact with your newly created Kubernetes cluster using kubectl.

```bash

linode-cli lke kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --format yaml > ~/.kube/config

```

> This command retrieves the kubeconfig for your cluster and saves it.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the list of nodes with their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/linode/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/metrics-server/master/deploy/1.8+/metrics-server-deployment.yaml

```

> This command installs the cluster autoscaler for Linode.
> This command installs the metrics server for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli lke cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

uninstall.title: Remove Kubeconfig
uninstall.description:
This step removes the kubeconfig file from your local machine.

```bash

rm ~/.kube/config

```

> This command deletes the kubeconfig file.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and check the current node status.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

linode-cli lke cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of your nodes before proceeding.
> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
> Verify that the nodes are updated successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly configured and the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient nodes available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough nodes to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count using 'linode-cli lke cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --node-count <new-count>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Linode API token is invalid or lacks permissions.

**Fix:**
Regenerate your Linode API token and ensure it has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified cluster name does not exist.

**Fix:**
Double-check the cluster name and ensure it matches the one created.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features in Kubernetes 1.30, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with their statuses as 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Linode CLI installed and configured with your API token.
